message:
docs: clarify legacy kernel source corpus is gitignored

The legacy_ext4_and_btrfs_code/ directory was referenced as if present
in the repo, but it's actually gitignored due to size (~205K lines).

Updated all documentation to clarify:
- Original kernel source is from Linux v6.19 at kernel.org
- Behavioral contracts are fully captured in EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md
- Local corpus is gitignored, not missing

Closes bd-2oag

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2oa","title":"ffs-error: Decide mount-validation error variants (UnsupportedFeature/UnsupportedBlockSize/etc)","description":"Context: mount validation can fail because of unsupported feature bits, block sizes, or incompatible on-disk geometry.

Question: do we represent these as:
- ParseError::InvalidField (and map to FfsError::Format), OR
- dedicated FfsError variants (UnsupportedFeature, UnsupportedBlockSize, InvalidGeometry), OR
- an intermediate MountValidationError used by ffs-core.

Deliverables:
- Chosen approach documented.
- Error mapping yields correct errno (EINVAL vs EOPNOTSUPP) for FUSE and CLI.

Acceptance:
- ext4 validate_v1() failures are surfaced with stable errors and good UX.
- Docs/spec do not list non-existent FfsError variants.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AzureBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:14:59.187355332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T15:53:56.423539784Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T15:53:56.423519876Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation"]}

 {"id":"bd-2oah","title":"EPIC: Parallel WAL — Silo/Aether Style (Graveyard Entry 15.4)","description":"# EPIC: Parallel WAL — Silo/Aether Style (Graveyard Entry 15.4)

## PURPOSE
Scale WAL (Write-Ahead Log) throughput for concurrent MVCC writers by using per-core WAL buffers with epoch-based ordering. Current single WAL is a serialization bottleneck for concurrent transactions.

## BACKGROUND
From the Alien CS Graveyard entry 15.4:
- Silo (Tu et al., SOSP 2013): per-core log buffers, epoch-based commit ordering
- Aether (Johnson et al., VLDB 2010): decoupled log buffer management, early lock release
- Key insight: WAL serialization is unnecessary if epoch ordering provides equivalent guarantees

## DESIGN

### Per-Core WAL Buffers
- Each CPU core has a dedicated WAL buffer (thread-local or core-affinity)
- Transactions write to their core's buffer without contention
- No central lock on WAL append

### Epoch-Based Ordering
- Global epoch counter advanced periodically (e.g., every 10ms or every N commits)
- Each WAL entry stamped with current epoch
- Durability guarantee: all entries in epoch E are durable before epoch E+1 begins
- Recovery: replay all entries in epoch order (entries within same epoch are commutative)

### Group Commit
- All WAL buffers for epoch E flushed together (group commit)
- Single fsync for all cores' buffers at epoch boundary
- Reduces fsync overhead from O(transactions) to O(epochs)

### Recovery
- On crash: find last complete epoch across all per-core logs
- Replay all entries up to last complete epoch
- Discard entries from incomplete epoch (they never committed)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Per-core WAL buffers eliminate contention
- [ ] Epoch-based ordering provides equivalent durability guarantees
- [ ] Group commit reduces fsync frequency
- [ ] Recovery correctly handles incomplete epochs
- [ ] Benchmark: WAL throughput scales with core count

## Success Criteria
1. All children closed
2. E2E WAL test (bd-2oah.4) passes
3. Parallel WAL scales linearly to 4+ cores
4. Group commit reduces fsync count by >= 50pct
5. Crash recovery replays all committed transactions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:24:54.753437422Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T01:11:46.641784818Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T01:11:46.641711741Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["concurrency","durability","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oah","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:40.817042970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":99,"issue_id":"bd-2oah","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW (lint fix): Epic is missing ## Success Criteria section. Add measurable success criteria before any children are marked closed. Example format: '## Success Criteria\n1. All children closed\n2. E2E test suite passes\n3. Feature parity gate green'","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:42Z"},{"id":175,"issue_id":"bd-2oah","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: concurrency_perf
Mapped graveyard sections: 14.10 EBR + 15.1 S3-FIFO + 15.4 Parallel WAL
EV score: 13.333333333333334 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: max_chain_len=64, wal_flush_batch=256, dirty_ratio_cap=0.8
Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

Required proof artifacts for execution on this bead:
- Baseline + hotspot evidence before changes.
- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2oah.1","title":"Design per-core WAL buffer for concurrent MVCC writes","description":"# Design per-core WAL buffer for concurrent MVCC writes

## GOAL
Design and implement per-core WAL buffers that eliminate contention on the write-ahead log for concurrent MVCC transactions.

## DESIGN

### Buffer Structure
- Thread-local WAL buffer: Vec<WalEntry> per core
- Core affinity: use thread_local! or core_affinity crate
- Buffer capacity: configurable (default 4MB per core)
- When buffer full: flush to disk, reset

### WAL Entry Format
```rust
struct WalEntry {
    epoch: u64,
    txn_id: TxnId,
    entry_type: WalEntryType,
    payload: Vec<u8>,
    crc32c: u32,
}
```

### Concurrency
- No locks on per-core buffers (thread-local access)
- Only contention point: epoch counter increment (atomic CAS)
- Flush can happen concurrently across cores

### Integration
- Replace single WAL in ffs-mvcc with per-core buffer pool
- TxnManager routes writes to current core's buffer
- Epoch manager coordinates global epoch advancement

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Per-core buffers avoid contention (no mutex on hot path)
- [ ] Buffer management handles full/flush correctly
- [ ] Core affinity works on Linux (sched_setaffinity or equivalent)
- [ ] Unit tests: concurrent writes to separate buffers
- [ ] Benchmark: throughput vs single WAL baseline","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralReef","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:05.486513103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:33:55.958516141Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:33:55.958434879Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oah.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2oah","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:05.486513103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":84,"issue_id":"bd-2oah.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: This bead is missing a ## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS section. Every implementation bead must specify structured log events at trace/debug/info/warn/error levels with field names. Add logging spec before implementation begins.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:18Z"},{"id":174,"issue_id":"bd-2oah.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: max_chain_len=64, wal_flush_batch=256, dirty_ratio_cap=0.8
Fallback trigger: single-writer conservative mode with bounded queue

- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:27Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2oah.2","title":"Implement epoch-based WAL ordering","description":"# Implement epoch-based WAL ordering

## GOAL
Implement the epoch mechanism that provides ordering guarantees across per-core WAL buffers without requiring a global serialization point.

## DESIGN

### Epoch Manager
```rust
struct EpochManager {
    current_epoch: AtomicU64,
    epoch_interval: Duration,     // e.g., 10ms
    commit_threshold: u64,        // advance after N commits
}
```

### Epoch Advancement
- Advance epoch when: interval expires OR commit_threshold reached (whichever first)
- On advance: atomically increment current_epoch
- All new WAL entries stamped with new epoch
- Old epoch entries become eligible for group commit

### Durability Contract
- Transaction T in epoch E is durable after epoch E is fully flushed
- Transaction commit returns only after its epoch is flushed
- Callers waiting for durability block on epoch flush (not individual entry flush)

### Recovery Ordering
- Epochs are globally ordered
- Within an epoch, entries are commutative (no ordering required)
- Recovery: for each epoch in order, apply all entries

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Epoch advances correctly (time-based and count-based triggers)
- [ ] WAL entries correctly stamped with epoch
- [ ] Durability contract holds: committed entries survive crash
- [ ] Recovery respects epoch ordering
- [ ] Unit tests: epoch advance timing, entry stamping","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralReef","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:16.063104850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T00:41:13.950975111Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T00:41:13.950886515Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["epoch","wal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oah.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2oah","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:25:16.063104850Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2oah.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2oah.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:31:29.355925517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":85,"issue_id":"bd-2oah.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SECOND-PASS REVIEW: This bead is missing a ## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS section. Every implementation bead must specify structured log events at trace/debug/info/warn/error levels with field names. Add logging spec before implementation begins.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:04:18Z"},{"id":173,"issue_id":"bd-2oah.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 ## 1. Mission

+Port legacy ext4 + btrfs behavior from Linux kernel v6.19 into a memory-safe Rust workspace (`FrankenFS`) with:
 
 - mount-compatible semantics for in-scope features,
 - MVCC/COW internals,
 - self-healing durability strategy.
 
 ## 2. Source of Truth and Inputs
 
-- Legacy source corpus:
-  - `legacy_ext4_and_btrfs_code/linux-fs/fs/ext4`
-  - `legacy_ext4_and_btrfs_code/linux-fs/fs/btrfs`

+  - `fs/btrfs` (btrfs filesystem implementation)

-## 3. Clarification: `legacy_glib_code`

-The repository currently contains `legacy_ext4_and_btrfs_code` and does **not** contain a separate `legacy_glib_code` path.
-

The behavioral contracts were extracted from Linux v6.19 and are captured in [EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md](EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md). For the original source, see `git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git` at tag v6.19.
 
 ## 4. Explicit Exclusions (Current Iteration)

@@ -1374,7 +1374,7 @@ See [COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md](COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1
 - **Kernel FUSE writeback-cache mode is intentionally unsupported in V1.x.** The epoch barrier design is implemented and crash-tested, but `writeback_cache` is not enabled in mount options. `flush` is a non-durability lifecycle hook; `fsync` / `fsyncdir` are the explicit durability boundaries.
 - **Default CLI mount path does not enable optional backpressure/per-core scheduling hooks.** `ffs-cli mount` currently uses the standard `ffs-fuse` mount path without wiring `BackpressureGate` controls.
 - **External dependencies.** Workspace dependencies currently use crates.io releases (`asupersync = 0.2.5`, `ftui = 0.2.1`); local path overrides can be supplied with Cargo `[patch]` during sibling-repo development.
-- **Legacy reference corpus is checked in.** The Linux kernel ext4/btrfs source used for behavioral extraction is available under `legacy_ext4_and_btrfs_code/linux-fs/`. The extracted behavior is fully captured in [EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md](EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md).
+- **Legacy reference corpus is not included.** The Linux kernel ext4/btrfs source used for behavioral extraction (~205K lines) is gitignored due to size. The extracted behavioral contracts are fully captured in [EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md](EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md). For the original source, see `git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git` at tag v6.19.

 - **btrfs**: B-tree, transaction, delayed refs, scrub, extent allocation

+The original kernel source is not included (gitignored due to size); for reference, use `git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git` at tag v6.19. All extracted behavioral contracts are captured in [EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md](EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md) (95KB).
 
 ---
